title: 有翼之暗
summary:
The narrator confronts the silent figure behind the coffin, revealing that the housekeeper, Nishimura, is actually the ninety-year-old Lady Hinata. The narrator explains that the real Nishimura was placed in a coffin two years ago, allowing Hinata to assume her identity through heavy makeup, long bangs, and her resemblance to her daughter, Tsukiyomi. Hinata had kept Tsukiyomi locked in the basement for over twenty years as part of an elaborate deception.

Hinata orchestrated a series of manipulations to frame Mistuho for the crimes. She utilized a scheme involving the "Country of Origin" book series, knowing that Mistuho, a non-Japanese person, would not realize that a specific book's title had been changed to "The Gate of Life and Death" due to the Pacific War. Additionally, Hinata poisoned Oyama with arsenic while disguised as Nishimura. She later manipulated Mitsutaka into opening Oyama's coffin to view a decapitated corpse, which prevented anyone from suspecting that Oyama was actually still alive.

To further the deception, Hinata manipulated the detective Wakizaki by forging a letter from Aoi to prompt his investigation. She planted false clues, such as a foreign LP record and citron pips, to lead Wakizaki to the false conclusion that Fujiko was the murderer. Finally, the narrator reveals that the "locked-room mystery" of the "Hell Gate" was an illusion; Hinata simply used a spare key kept in Yuki's room, meaning the perceived impossibility of the crime was merely the result of investigators overcomplicating a simple act of theft and murder.
